excluded_defs.name: dom_parser_implementation
excluded_defs.reason: Read to understand structural_indexes and next_structural_index field layout; not directly edited

exploration_log.aha_moment: The `internal::dom_parser_implementation` already has `next_structural_index` and `structural_indexes[]` that together give the byte offset of the last processed structural token — perfect for reporting an error location.
exploration_log.hindsight: For ondemand, most errors are lazy so error_location() only reflects stage1 failures. Could have documented this more prominently in the header.

alternatives: Store a raw pointer to the error position in the input buffer
key_decisions.decision: Add _error_location field to both parsers, populated from structural_indexes after stage1/parse failure
key_decisions.reasoning: Symmetric API between DOM and on-demand; using byte offset (size_t) matches the existing capacity() and similar APIs; structural_indexes already stores these offsets

search_sequence.reasoning: Task says to add method to parser classes; need to understand existing API and fields
search_sequence.result: DOM parser has deprecated error field; ondemand parser has _capacity/_max_capacity/_max_depth fields

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to find what data is available after a parse failure to construct a byte offset
search_sequence.result: Found structural_indexes[], next_structural_index, n_structural_indexes — structural_indexes[idx] is the byte offset

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to find where parse fails to inject the error location capture
search_sequence.result: DOM: parse_into_document() calls implementation->parse(); ondemand: iterate() calls implementation->stage1()

queries.query_text: Add error_location() method to dom/parser.h and ondemand/parser.h that returns byte offset from structural_indexes after parse failure

solve_notes: Added `error_location()` to both `dom::parser` and `ondemand::parser`. Each stores a `_error_location` field (default 0) populated from `implementation->structural_indexes[next_structural_index]` after parse or stage-1 failure. The method is declared in the respective `.h` files and implemented in the corresponding `-inl.h` files.
task_text: When parsing fails, the `error_code` returned by `dom::parser::parse()` or `ondemand::parser::iterate()` does not include the byte offset where the error was detected. Add an `error_location()` method to parser classes that returns the byte offset within the input buffer where the last error occurred, enabling callers to produce "error at byte 1234" diagnostics.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: structural_indexes
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the uint32_t[] array whose entries are byte offsets; needed to understand how to extract the error byte offset

thrash_preventing_defs.name: next_structural_index
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the current index into structural_indexes after parsing; paired with structural_indexes to compute the error byte offset

thrash_preventing_defs.name: n_structural_indexes
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: bounds for the structural_indexes array; needed to guard against out-of-bounds access

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the edited defs. The thrash_preventing set adds the three fields from dom_parser_implementation that an AI agent would need to look up to understand how to compute the error byte offset — without them, the agent would search for 'how does simdjson track position' before finding these fields.
